question_title: Will the interest in “reza pahlavi” change between 2026-03-02 and 2026-03-11 according to Google Trends?
summary: The Google Trends value for "reza pahlavi" on Mar 2 is 100 — the absolute peak of the resolution window — driven by the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (Feb 28) and Pahlavi's high-profile 60 Minutes interview (Mar 1). The threshold arithmetic is decisive: with a start value of 100, "Increases" requires >103 (impossible without rescaling from a new higher peak), "Doesn't change" requires 97-100 (near-peak sustained interest), and "Decreases" requires <97 (any meaningful decay). Given that this is a curiosity-driven person search ("who is Reza Pahlavi?") that will naturally decay over 9 days even with the ongoing Iran crisis, Decreases is the overwhelmingly dominant outcome. The only scenarios for non-Decrease outcomes require Pahlavi to become an even bigger story (e.g., returning to Iran, officially recognized as leader) specifically near Mar 11.

factors.description: Mar 2 value is 100 (peak of the entire window). For 'Doesn't change', Mar 11 must be 97-100; for 'Increases', Mar 11 must exceed 103 (impossible unless rescaling occurs). The asymmetric possibility space overwhelmingly favors Decreases.

The spike is driven by a one-time curiosity burst ('who is Reza Pahlavi?') from the 60 Minutes interview (Mar 1) + Khamenei assassination (Feb 28). Rising query '60 minutes reza pahlavi' at 99,500% confirms this. Curiosity-driven person searches decay rapidly after initial exposure.

Historical mini-spike in this data: Feb 15 peak at 15 decayed to 6 next day, then 4. Even smaller spikes show rapid decay. This massive spike will follow the same pattern with even more pronounced decay in absolute terms.

Active ongoing geopolitical crisis: Iran leadership succession crisis is unresolved, US military operations continuing, Pahlavi positioning as transitional leader. This could generate new Pahlavi-specific news that sustains or re-spikes interest.

For 'Increases' to resolve, a new peak day (Mar 3-10) must rescale Mar 2 below Mar 11. This requires Pahlavi to become an even bigger story (e.g., enters Iran, officially recognized as leader). Possible given the active crisis but requires specific Pahlavi-focused developments, not just general Iran news.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (87%), Increases (7%), Doesn't change (6%)**

Key reasoning:

- **Starting at 100 (the peak)** makes this heavily asymmetric. Any decay at all — even to 96 — resolves as "Decreases." The bar for "Doesn't change" (97-100) or "Increases" (>103) is extremely high.

- **The spike is curiosity-driven**, triggered by Khamenei's assassination (Feb 28) and Pahlavi's 60 Minutes interview (Mar 1). The rising query "60 minutes reza pahlavi" at 99,500% confirms this was a "who is this person?" search burst, which decays fast.

- **Even with the ongoing Iran crisis**, the specific person-search for "reza pahlavi" will decline as coverage shifts to broader Iran topics. By Mar 11 (9 days later), the initial curiosity wave will have substantially faded.

- **The only path to non-Decrease outcomes** requires a dramatic new Pahlavi-specific development (returning to Iran, official recognition as transitional leader) that generates a second spike near Mar 11 — possible given the active crisis but unlikely to exceed the initial burst.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “reza pahlavi” change between 2026-03-02 and 2026-03-11 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42394)
